quotes: mark sits at the lightest end

relations.note: Specifies the orientation, progression, and logo placement for the approved masthead gradient.

quotes: Gradient Specifications (approved set — never invent gradients)
Brand Blue → Dark Navy | `#00529b` → `#011E45` | Dark backgrounds, blue masthead/waves
Sky Blue → Deep Blue | `#358CCB` → `#003D74` | Blue backgrounds, waves
Golden Sand → White → Light Blue | `#FEF1C8` → `#ffffff` → `#E6F0F9` | Soft light backgrounds
Golden Sand → 75% Gold → Sunshine | `#FEF1C8` → Gold @75% → `#faa31b` | Warm light-to-saturated backgrounds
Sunshine → 0C,10M,78Y,0K (≈`#ffe05c` light gold) | `#faa31b` → light gold | **Masthead gradient — the gradient to use with the logo mark for contrast; mark sits at the lightest end**
Used as backgrounds, in combination with the wave graphic, and with the LISRAYA logo mark. Approved values only.

rules.intent: Keep gradient treatments within the approved brand color system and intended usage categories.
rules.rule_text: Never invent gradients. Use only the approved recipes: Brand Blue → Dark Navy for dark backgrounds and blue mastheads or waves; Sky Blue → Deep Blue for blue backgrounds and waves; Sunshine → 75% Gold for warm backgrounds; Golden Sand → White → Light Blue for soft light backgrounds; Golden Sand → 75% Gold → Sunshine for warm light-to-saturated backgrounds; and Sunshine → light gold for mastheads. Approved gradients are background treatments that may be combined with the wave graphic and LISRAYA logo mark.

quotes: Masthead gradient — the gradient to use with the logo mark for contrast
Gradients run horizontally in masthead/banner applications
Masthead gradient — the gradient to use with the logo mark for contrast; mark sits at the lightest end
Gradients run horizontally in masthead/banner applications (dark/saturated → light, left → right as shown).

rules.intent: Preserve the approved gradient progression and maintain logo contrast.
rules.rule_text: Masthead and banner gradients run horizontally from dark or saturated at the left to light at the right. The masthead uses the Sunshine → light-gold gradient, and the LISRAYA logo mark sits at its lightest end for contrast.
